_meta.note: Migrated from profile_bank.py. Ground truth for all agent claims about Raphael's career and background.

education.notes: CGPA 4.98/6.0 (6.0 = excellent). 75/75 ECTS. Self-funded. Triple-accredited (EQUIS, AACSB, AMBA). Ranked #6 in Europe by FT 2021. LAW (Learning Assessment Week): 24-hour consulting case presented to professors and industry panel.

education.institution: IFMA (now SIGMA) — French Institute for Advanced Mechanics
education.notes: École des Mines group. Specialization: Machines, Mechanisms and Systems.

education.institution: Lycée La Martinière Monplaisir — Classes Préparatoires (CPGE)
education.degree: Intensive 2-year preparatory course for competitive engineering school entrance
education.notes: Signals academic selectivity and rigour in French context.

experience.summary: Senior Associate in the Deals Strategy practice. Generalist across sectors, Swiss mid-market focus. Full deal lifecycle: CDD, VDD, carve-out advisory, PMI, value creation, 100-day planning. Trilingual client work (EN/FR/DE). Manages workstreams independently; coordinates 2–3 junior analysts on faster-paced engagements. 10+ engagements since Nov 2022.

achievements.text: Delivered 10+ M&A engagements across the full deal lifecycle — from pre-deal market screening to post-acquisition integration — for PE and corporate clients in the Swiss mid-market and internationally.

Led and owned CDD workstreams independently: market sizing & attractiveness, customer & revenue analysis, competitor benchmarking, voice of customer, synergy assessment, operational performance review, and company benchmarking against sector peers.
Conducted management interviews as part of due diligence processes, synthesizing qualitative intelligence into investment-grade findings.

Authored and contributed to final deliverables including full CDD/VDD reports, executive red flag memos, carve-out perimeter & separation plans, value creation roadmaps, and board-level presentations.
Advised on carve-out situations — defining separation perimeters, interdependencies, and standalone cost structures for divested entities.
Built 100-day value creation plans translating strategic investment thesis into concrete operational priorities and quick wins.
Assessed distressed and turnaround situations under time pressure, evaluating recovery levers and viability for PE clients.
Operated across English, French, and German depending on client context.
Mentored and coordinated junior analysts on select engagements, owning quality and output under tight deal timelines.
Applied AI tooling (Claude Code, Codex, Python) to automate repetitive deliverable tasks — cutting production time on structured outputs and enabling faster iteration on client-facing documents.

experience.summary: Generalist Swiss-focused early-stage fund (Pre-Seed to Series A). Joined as intern, promoted to full-time Venture Scout / Analyst within the year. Sole owner of initial screening process; part of 3–4 person analyst team for deep-dive analysis.

achievements.text: Sole owner of the initial screening process — independently evaluated 60+ startup applications, applying a consistent scoring framework across business model, team, market, and traction dimensions.
Contributed to 5+ investments across Pre-Seed, Seed, and Series A stages, including deals sourced directly through own network and outreach.
Built and maintained a diversified deal flow pipeline across all major Swiss sourcing channels: accelerators, incubators, university spin-outs, founder referrals, demo days, and cold inbound.
Conducted deep-dive investment analysis including financial modelling, valuation review, market sizing (TAM/SAM/SOM), and competitive landscape mapping.
Authored Investment Memoranda presented to the investment committee.
Promoted from intern to full-time Venture Scout / Analyst within the same year on the basis of performance.

experience.summary: Role created specifically for Raphael. Dual mandate: (1) build a cost/savings calculation tool from scratch for all new manufacturing projects in the fournitures division (sub-millimeter watch movement components); (2) drive full organizational adoption across a 600+ person workforce spanning every level of the hierarchy, with no formal authority.

achievements.text: Designed and built an object-oriented cost modelling tool to calculate and track costs and savings for every new production project in the fournitures division — enabling management accounting decisions previously performed manually or not at all.
Contributed to identifying and realizing CHF 1–5M in savings during tenure, providing C-level visibility into project economics at an unprecedented granularity.

Drove adoption of the tool across 600+ employees — operators, industrialization engineers, finance controllers, operations managers, and C-suite — navigating significant organizational resistance at every level of the hierarchy.
Managed a politically complex change management programme with no formal authority, relying entirely on persuasion, relationship-building, and demonstrated value to move a resistant organization.
Operated as the connective tissue between site C-level leadership and workshop-floor operators, translating financial logic into operational language and vice versa.

experience.summary: Two-year role within the fournitures division — internal service manufacturing sub-millimeter watch movement components to micrometer tolerances. Grew from junior contributor to full project ownership across measurement infrastructure for a new production line.

achievements.text: Developed a Java/JavaScript tool to eliminate redundant remeasurement cycles on 3D coordinate measuring machines (CMMs), saving thousands of hours per year across the production line.

Contributed to the design and deployment of a full measurement infrastructure for a new fournitures production line — defining control points, measurement protocols, and quality gates for watch movement components at micrometer precision.
Managed computer vision projects to increase measurement throughput and compliance with Rolex's operational excellence standards.

experience.summary: Conditional inclusion only — luxury / manufacturing / industrial roles. CNC programming and machining for luxury watch and jewelry components.

achievements.text: CNC programming and machining for luxury watch and jewellery components.
Industrialization of new parts and contributed to machine improvement initiatives on the production floor.

tools: Claude / ChatGPT (daily professional use, prompt engineering)

conditional_mentions.reason: Short internship at Chopard. Only surfaces when luxury / precision manufacturing / industrial sector relevance is high. Omit entirely for strategy, finance, or tech roles.
Mention only when space allows and role is France-facing. Signals academic selectivity in French context only.
PPL(A) licence worth surfacing for roles valuing precision, rule-following under pressure, and technical rigour. ATPL studies ongoing.

framing.explanation: Role exp_rolex_1 (Rolex Control Methods, Mar 2015–Jul 2017) must NEVER be described as an internship. Use the actual title: 'Project Manager, Control Methods Department'.
Never write the word 'VBA'. Describe the tool's function or use 'object-oriented programming'.
Never show individual course grades. Show only overall CGPA: 4.98/6.0 for HSG MBA.
Output must always fit a single page. Apply ruthless selection — trim bullets before expanding.
No bullet or sentence should open with: leveraged, utilized, spearheaded, harnessed, fostered, championed. Vary openers and lengths.
Banned words anywhere in output: leveraged, utilized, spearheaded, harnessed, fostered, championed, passionate, excited, thrilled, eager, dynamic, synergies (as verb).
Mirror job ad language exactly for ATS keyword matching. Use the JD's own terminology where applicable.
Use 'Venture Scout / Analyst' not just 'Analyst' or 'Intern'. Reflects promotion within the year.

tense.note: Roles ending on or after 2026-05-31 use present tense, including PwC (contract_ending). Never write 'left PwC' or similar until after departure date.

cover_letter_reference.note: Actual cover letter written by Raphael (Pilatus, March 2026). Use as voice and structure reference — not as content to copy.
structure_observed: Para 1: direct application statement + 2-3 domain profile formula + specific motivation for this company (aviation passion for Pilatus)
Para 2: current role at PwC described concretely (3 activities) + explicit mapping sentence naming JD requirements ('Diese Erfahrung passt zu... insbesondere in Bezug auf...')
Para 3: prior role at Rolex, transition word 'Zuvor', 3 action verbs, closes with 'Diese Erfahrung hat meine Fähigkeit gestärkt, [specific competency]'
Para 4: education in one sentence (MBA HSG + Maschinenbau) + specific motivation for THIS company (not generic)
Para 5: 2 sentences — company values (brief) + desire to contribute
Para 6: formal German close ('Vielen Dank... Ich freue mich auf ein persönliches Gespräch')
voice_notes: Tone is formal but direct — no preamble, each sentence carries information
JD mapping is made explicit, not left implicit
Company-specific motivation is tied to a real reason (aviation passion, sector, technology) — never invented
No employment status mentioned in closing
German register: 'mit grossem Interesse', 'Leidenschaft für [X] with real reason', formal close are conventional and acceptable
